prompt: Write two sentences describing how it feels when someone asks you the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer.
response: When someone asks you the same question multiple times, rephrasing it six different ways in an attempt to elicit a different response, it can be frustrating and feel like they're not listening to your initial answer or worse, trying to manipulate you into saying something you don't mean. The repetition can also start to feel condescending or like an interrogation, making you defensive and even more resolute in your original response, as if you're being worn down by the persistence of their questioning.
